source.title: MM-809 / GitHub issue 809: Add high security mode
source.originalDesign:
Add a high security mode that scans outgoing data before sending it for secrets or credentials:
* comments before posting
* git commits before push
* messages before send message

coverageGate: PASS - every major design point is owned by at least one story.
designSummary: MM-809 requests an operator-visible high security mode that scans outbound content before it leaves MoonMind-controlled workflows or runtimes. The declared outbound surfaces are comments before posting, git commits before push, and messages before send message. The common intent is pre-send secret or credential detection with a consistent enforcement contract that downstream surface-specific stories can reuse.

coveragePoints.title: High security mode
coveragePoints.explanation: MoonMind must expose a high security mode rather than permanently forcing the behavior into every run.

coveragePoints.title: Pre-send outbound scanning
coveragePoints.explanation: Outgoing data must be scanned before it is sent, posted, or pushed.

coveragePoints.title: Secret and credential detection
coveragePoints.explanation: The scan target is secrets or credentials in outbound data.

coveragePoints.title: Comment posting protection
coveragePoints.explanation: Comment publication paths must scan content before posting.

coveragePoints.title: Git push protection
coveragePoints.source_section: Issue bullet: git commits before push
coveragePoints.explanation: Git commit content must be scanned before push operations are allowed.

coveragePoints.title: Message send protection
coveragePoints.source_section: Issue bullet: messages before send message
coveragePoints.explanation: Message-sending paths must scan message content before sending.

coveragePoints.title: Fail-closed enforcement result
coveragePoints.explanation: When high security mode detects a likely secret or credential, the outbound operation needs a deterministic blocked result with actionable diagnostics instead of silently sending the data.

stories.summary: Orchestrate Phase 1: High security mode outbound scan contract
stories.description: As an operator, I need a high security mode and shared outbound scan contract so every protected surface can decide before send, post, or push whether data contains secrets or credentials. Full MM-809 plan context: enable a high security mode that scans outgoing data before sending it for secrets or credentials across comments before posting, git commits before push, and messages before send message.

sourceReference.title: MM-809 / GitHub issue 809: Add high security mode

scope: Define the high security mode switch and configuration precedence.
Define the shared outbound scan input, result, and blocked-operation diagnostics contract.
Provide deterministic secret and credential detection behavior suitable for comments, commits, and messages.
outOfScope: Surface-specific comment posting, git push, and message send wiring.
Provider-specific secret revocation or credential rotation.
stories.independentTest: Enable high security mode in a unit or boundary test, pass representative clean and secret-like outbound payloads through the shared scan contract, and verify clean payloads are allowed while detected credentials produce a fail-closed blocked result with redacted diagnostics.
acceptanceCriteria: A high security mode setting is available to runtime code with documented deterministic precedence.
Outbound scan callers receive a structured allow/block result before performing an external side effect.
Secret-like or credential-like values produce a blocked result when high security mode is enabled.
Diagnostics identify the category and location of the finding without echoing the raw secret value.
When high security mode is disabled, the contract does not silently mutate outbound content.
requirements: Expose a high security mode flag to outbound side-effect code.
Create a reusable outbound scan contract for text payloads and commit-like payload bundles.
Fail closed when findings are detected under high security mode.
Redact any detected secret material from logs, artifacts, errors, and user-visible diagnostics.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns the mode itself and its runtime availability.
Owns the shared pre-send decision point used by every later phase.
Owns the common secret and credential detection semantics.
Owns fail-closed blocked-result behavior and redacted diagnostics.

assumptions: High security mode is operator-controlled rather than always-on.
The first implementation may use deterministic pattern-based scanning before later provider-specific detectors are added.
needsClarification: Which configuration surface should expose high security mode first: environment, profile setting, Mission Control UI, or all of them?

stories.summary: Orchestrate Phase 2: Scan comments before posting
stories.description: As an operator, I need MoonMind to scan comments before posting them so high security mode prevents accidental publication of secrets or credentials to GitHub, Jira, or other comment destinations. Full MM-809 plan context: enable a high security mode that scans outgoing data before sending it for secrets or credentials across comments before posting, git commits before push, and messages before send message.

sourceReference.title: MM-809 / GitHub issue 809: Add high security mode

scope: Route comment publication payloads through the shared outbound scan contract.
Block comment posting when high security mode detects a secret or credential.
Return a redacted actionable error to the workflow, adapter, or trusted tool caller.
outOfScope: Git commit or message send enforcement.
Changing comment formatting behavior unrelated to security scanning.
stories.independentTest: Exercise a trusted comment-posting path with high security mode enabled and verify a clean comment is posted or reaches the mocked provider, while a comment containing a representative credential is blocked before the provider call.
acceptanceCriteria: All MoonMind-owned comment-posting code paths covered by the story call the outbound scan contract before provider submission.
Detected secrets or credentials prevent the provider comment API call.
Blocked comment attempts emit redacted diagnostics that identify the comment surface.
Clean comments continue through the existing publication path without content mutation.
requirements: Identify the canonical comment-posting boundaries that perform external side effects.
Invoke the shared high security scan before each comment side effect.
Preserve existing success behavior for clean comments.
Add boundary tests proving no provider call occurs after a blocked scan.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Applies the pre-send scan requirement to comment publishing.
Verifies comment content is scanned for secrets or credentials.
Owns the explicit comments-before-posting issue bullet.
Enforces fail-closed behavior for blocked comment posts.

assumptions: Comment posting includes any MoonMind trusted tool, adapter, or publish path that sends user-visible comments to an external service.
needsClarification: Which comment destinations must be included in the first phase: GitHub, Jira, PR review comments, issue comments, or all MoonMind-owned comment publishers?

stories.summary: Orchestrate Phase 3: Scan git commits before push
stories.description: As an operator, I need MoonMind to scan git commit content before pushing so high security mode stops credentials from leaving the workspace through git history. Full MM-809 plan context: enable a high security mode that scans outgoing data before sending it for secrets or credentials across comments before posting, git commits before push, and messages before send message.

sourceReference.title: MM-809 / GitHub issue 809: Add high security mode
sections: Issue bullet: git commits before push

scope: Determine the commit range that would be pushed by MoonMind-controlled git operations.
Scan commit messages and changed file content or diffs before push.
Block push attempts with redacted diagnostics when findings are detected.
outOfScope: Rewriting git history or automatically removing secrets.
Scanning remote repositories outside the commits MoonMind is about to push.
stories.independentTest: Create a temporary repository fixture with one clean commit and one commit containing a representative credential, invoke the MoonMind-controlled push boundary in high security mode with the provider call mocked, and verify the dirty range is blocked before push while the clean range proceeds.
acceptanceCriteria: MoonMind-controlled git push paths scan the outbound commit range before invoking push.
Commit messages and changed content are included in the scan input or an explicitly justified equivalent.
Detected secrets or credentials prevent the git push command or provider push call.
Blocked push diagnostics identify affected commit/file locations without printing raw secret material.
Clean commit ranges continue through the existing push path.
requirements: Resolve the outbound commit range for push operations deterministically.
Build scan payloads from commit metadata and changed content with size controls if needed.
Invoke the shared high security scan before push.
Add tests proving blocked scans do not execute git push.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Applies the pre-send scan requirement to git push operations.
Verifies outbound commit data is scanned for secrets or credentials.
Owns the explicit git-commits-before-push issue bullet.
Enforces fail-closed behavior for blocked pushes.

assumptions: The first enforcement target is MoonMind-owned push execution, not arbitrary manual git commands run outside MoonMind control.
needsClarification: Should the initial scan inspect full file contents, diffs only, commit messages only plus diffs, or a configured combination?

stories.summary: Orchestrate Phase 4: Scan messages before send
stories.description: As an operator, I need MoonMind to scan outbound messages before send message actions so high security mode prevents credentials from being sent through agent, chat, notification, or provider-message surfaces. Full MM-809 plan context: enable a high security mode that scans outgoing data before sending it for secrets or credentials across comments before posting, git commits before push, and messages before send message.

sourceReference.title: MM-809 / GitHub issue 809: Add high security mode
sections: Issue bullet: messages before send message

scope: Route MoonMind-owned send-message payloads through the shared outbound scan contract.
Block message sending when high security mode detects a secret or credential.
Return redacted diagnostics to the calling runtime or workflow boundary.
outOfScope: Comment posting and git push enforcement.
Scanning inbound messages or private local draft state that is never sent.
stories.independentTest: Invoke a MoonMind-owned send-message boundary with high security mode enabled and a mocked downstream sender; verify clean messages reach the sender and messages containing representative credentials are blocked before the sender call.
acceptanceCriteria: MoonMind-owned send-message paths scan message content before calling the downstream sender.
Detected secrets or credentials prevent the send operation.
Blocked message attempts emit redacted diagnostics that identify the message surface.
Clean messages continue through the existing send path without content mutation.
requirements: Identify the canonical send-message boundary or boundaries covered by MM-809.
Invoke the shared high security scan before each message send side effect.
Preserve existing success behavior for clean messages.
Add boundary tests proving no downstream sender call occurs after a blocked scan.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Applies the pre-send scan requirement to send-message operations.
Verifies message content is scanned for secrets or credentials.
Owns the explicit messages-before-send-message issue bullet.
Enforces fail-closed behavior for blocked message sends.

assumptions: Message send refers to MoonMind-owned outbound message actions, not internal workflow state transitions.
needsClarification: Which send-message surfaces are in scope for the first implementation: Mission Control chat, agent runtime messages, notifications, or all MoonMind-owned outbound message senders?

dependencies.reason: Comment enforcement needs the shared mode and scan contract.
Git push enforcement needs the shared mode and scan contract.
Message enforcement needs the shared mode and scan contract.

outOfScope.rationale: MM-809 asks for pre-send scanning and prevention, not remediation of already exposed credentials.

outOfScope.item: Scanning arbitrary user actions outside MoonMind-controlled boundaries
outOfScope.rationale: The issue names outgoing MoonMind surfaces; unmanaged shell or provider activity requires a separate runtime containment design.

outOfScope.item: Creating specs, implementation plans, tasks, code, Jira issues, or PRs during breakdown
